talk_title: Plasma proteomic signature of persistent smoking effects and cancer risk

biomarkers.name: PSPSE proteomic signature score
biomarkers.finding: Higher score associated with increased risk of lung cancer (HR ~1.5) and improved PLCOm2012 model AUC, especially in long-term quitters

MOA_summary: The PSPSE proteomic signature captures persistent protein alterations after smoking cessation, reflecting cumulative exposure and biological memory, and its elevated levels predict higher incidence of smoking-related cancers, particularly lung cancer, independent of self-reported smoking metrics.
key_findings: Identified 18 proteins forming a PSPSE score that distinguishes current, former, and never smokers.
Higher PSPSE score significantly associated with increased lung cancer risk (HR ~1.5) in UK Biobank former smokers.
PSPSE score improves PLCOm2012 lung cancer risk prediction, especially in quitters >20 years.
Signature validated in an independent Asian cohort (Shanghai Men's Health Study) with consistent risk association.
open_questions: What are the functional roles of the 18 signature proteins in carcinogenesis?
Can the signature predict response to lung cancer screening or chemoprevention?

talk_title: Adult height genetics and fatal prostate cancer risk

speaker.name: Anqi Wang
speaker.affiliation: Harvard T.H. Chan School of Public Health

biomarkers.name: Height polygenic risk score
biomarkers.finding: PRS associated with fatal prostate cancer; residual height after adjusting for PRS shows no association

models.name: Health Professionals Follow-up Study
models.genotype_or_key_features: 51,000 men, DNA and tumor tissue available

clinical_data.metric: HR for fatal prostate cancer per SD increase in height PRS

MOA_summary: Genetically determined adult height, captured by a polygenic risk score, drives the observed association with fatal prostate cancer, likely via growth‑related pathways such as androgen signaling, rather than early‑life nutrition.
key_findings: Height PRS strongly associated with fatal prostate cancer (HR 1.30 per SD).
Residual height after accounting for PRS shows no association, indicating genetics drive the risk.
Androgen response pathway upregulated in tumors of taller men, linking height to prostate cancer biology.
open_questions: Can height PRS improve risk stratification beyond PSA screening?
What specific genetic variants within the PRS mediate androgen pathway activation?

talk_title: Copy number alteration subtypes in high‑grade serous ovarian cancer and racial survival disparities

models.name: ACES/NCSCS/NHS
models.genotype_or_key_features: 638 HSOC tumors with matched blood, 37% Black

clinical_data.metric: Hazard ratio for death (C1 loss‑dominant vs C2 CNA‑quiet)
Hazard ratio for death (Black vs White within gain‑dominant C3)

MOA_summary: Unsupervised clustering of genome‑wide copy‑number profiles from methylation arrays defines three HSOC subtypes (loss‑dominant, CNA‑quiet, gain‑dominant); CNA‑quiet tumors show higher immune infiltration and better survival, while Black patients in the gain‑dominant subtype experience higher mortality, suggesting ancestry‑linked biological contributors to disparity.
key_findings: Identified three CNA‑based clusters: loss‑dominant (44%), CNA‑quiet (40%), gain‑dominant (16%).
CNA‑quiet tumors have significantly higher immune cell infiltration and improved overall survival.
Within the gain‑dominant cluster, Black women have a 60% higher hazard of death compared to White women.
open_questions: Do specific copy‑number events drive the immune‑cold phenotype in gain‑dominant tumors?
Can targeted therapies reverse the survival disadvantage in Black patients with gain‑dominant HSOC?

talk_title: Spatial context of T‑cell infiltration and exhaustion predicts ovarian cancer survival in Black women

biomarkers.name: Cytotoxic T‑cell enrichment (tumor vs stroma)
biomarkers.finding: Tumor‑dominant enrichment associated with better overall survival (HR 0.68)

biomarkers.name: TIM‑3+ exhausted CD8+ T cells in tumor
biomarkers.finding: Presence associated with improved survival in tumors with ≥2% T‑cell infiltration

clinical_data.metric: HR for death per unit increase in tumor‑dominant cytotoxic gradient
HR for death with tumor TIM‑3+ exhaustion (≥2% T cells)

MOA_summary: Multiplex immunofluorescence of whole‑section ovarian tumors reveals that cytotoxic CD8+ T cells confer survival benefit when enriched in the tumor compartment versus stroma, and tumor‑localized TIM‑3+ exhausted T cells paradoxically mark better outcomes, indicating spatially resolved immune functionality drives prognosis in Black women.
key_findings: Tumor‑dominant cytotoxic T‑cell enrichment predicts improved survival (HR 0.68).
Stroma‑dominant cytotoxic enrichment predicts worse survival.
Tumor TIM‑3+ exhaustion associated with better survival among tumors with ≥2% T cells.
open_questions: Would checkpoint blockade efficacy differ by the identified spatial immunotypes?
Can spatial profiling be integrated into routine pathology for risk stratification?

talk_title: Pathways enriched in primary breast tumors with disseminated tumor cells

biomarkers.name: Wnt pathway mutations
biomarkers.finding: Associated with EMT signature and lower proliferation in DTC‑positive tumors

biomarkers.name: PI3K/AKT/mTOR mutations
biomarkers.finding: Linked to increased proliferative pathway expression and reduced apoptosis

models.name: Prospective breast cancer DTC cohort
models.genotype_or_key_features: 148 patients with RNA‑seq, WGS, bone‑marrow DTC assessment

MOA_summary: In early‑stage hormone‑receptor‑positive breast cancer, presence of disseminated tumor cells correlates with somatic mutations in Wnt, PI3K/AKT/mTOR, and NRF2 pathways; Wnt mutations drive EMT and a stem‑like migratory phenotype, while PI3K alterations promote proliferation and resistance, together facilitating dormancy and later metastasis.
key_findings: DTC‑positive tumors enriched for mutations in Wnt, PI3K/AKT/mTOR, and NRF2 pathways.
Wnt‑mutated DTC‑positive tumors show increased EMT and decreased proliferation signatures.
PI3K‑mutated DTC‑positive tumors display upregulated proliferative pathways and reduced apoptosis.
APOBEC mutagenesis activity nearly doubled in DTC‑positive patients.
open_questions: Can targeting Wnt or PI3K pathways eradicate DTCs and prevent recurrence?
What is the temporal relationship between DTC emergence and pathway activation?

talk_title: Colorectal cancer polygenic risk across birth cohorts

speaker.name: Xinyu Wang
speaker.affiliation: Harvard Chan School of Public Health

biomarkers.name: Colorectal cancer PRS (204 SNPs)
biomarkers.finding: Association with CRC risk strengthens in more recent birth cohorts (trend p<0.001)

clinical_data.metric: OR per SD increase in PRS

MOA_summary: A colorectal cancer polygenic risk score shows progressively stronger association with disease risk in later birth cohorts, suggesting gene‑environment interactions where modern lifestyle exposures amplify genetic susceptibility, particularly for distal colon and rectal cancers.
key_findings: PRS distribution unchanged across birth cohorts, but effect size increases in recent cohorts.
Stronger PRS‑CRC association observed in individuals <55 years, unscreened, without family history, and for distal colon/rectal cancers.
Innate immune system pathways enriched among SNPs showing birth‑cohort interaction.
open_questions: Which specific environmental factors (e.g., diet, obesity) drive the observed gene‑environment interaction?
Can integrating PRS with lifestyle scores improve early‑onset CRC screening?

talk_title: Functional characterization of STARD13 as a BRCA2 modifier in breast cancer

models.name: HMLE BRCA2 6174delT heterozygous
models.genotype_or_key_features: same mutation in mammary epithelial cells

MOA_summary: A non‑coding variant rs3742324 within an enhancer region regulates STARD13 expression; loss of STARD13 in BRCA2‑mutant breast epithelial cells amplifies EMT‑related genes (e.g., MMP9) and proliferative pathways, suggesting STARD13 acts as a genetic modifier of BRCA2‑associated breast cancer risk.
key_findings: rs3742324 region shows allele‑specific enhancer activity and regulates STARD13 expression.
CRISPR deletion of the enhancer reduces STARD13 transcript levels.
STARD13 knockdown leads to greater downregulation of LATAS2 in BRCA2‑mutant cells.
RNA‑seq reveals upregulation of EMT and extracellular matrix genes (e.g., MMP9) when STARD13 is reduced in BRCA2‑mutant background.
open_questions: Does STARD13 modulation affect sensitivity to PARP inhibitors in BRCA2‑mutant tumors?
Are there therapeutic strategies to restore STARD13 activity?
